1. Created folder "Csvtopostgres"
2. cd csv-to-postgres 
3. npm init -y
4. npm install pg-promise csv-parser
5. Create a file db.js

6. Prepare the CSV File "data.csv"
Ensure your CSV file (data.csv) has a structure compatible with your database table. Example CSV content:

id,name,email
1,Joe Donald,joe.donald@example.com
2,Janet South,janet.south@example.com
3,Samuel Wilder,sam.wilder@example.com

7. Create the Database Table "customers"
Run the following SQL script to create a table for the CSV data:

CREATE TABLE customers (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
);

8. Create a file index.js:

9. Run the Script
node index.js

DELETE FROM customers;

==============================================================

Neon is considered an SQL database, as it is a serverless implementation of PostgreSQL, which is a relational database that uses SQL for querying data; essentially, you interact with Neon using standard SQL commands through its SQL editor. 
Key points about Neon:
Based on PostgreSQL:
Neon is built on top of PostgreSQL, allowing you to use the same SQL syntax and functionality. 
Serverless architecture:
Neon provides a serverless experience, automatically scaling up and down based on usage. 
SQL editor:
Users can manage their Neon databases and run queries directly through an integrated SQL editor.
